story: I married Rose; four radiant years later she died after driving away angry. For a year I've gone daily to her grave with roses, kneeling, saying “I’m sorry. I love you.” The stone cannot hold all she was. At dawn, the groundskeeper taped a notice to the cemetery gate: “Due to storm-damaged limbs, the cemetery will close at noon today. Visitors may leave flowers at the office before 12.” Midmorning I sat staring at her wedding photo, keys and bouquet on the table, deciding when to go see her today.
implausible_knowledge: At dawn, the groundskeeper taped a notice to the cemetery gate: “Due to storm-damaged limbs, the cemetery will close at noon today. Visitors may leave flowers at the office before 12.”
question: When deciding when to go to the cemetery today, what will he most plausibly do?
answer_with_knowledge: Go immediately to leave the roses at the cemetery office before the noon closure.
answer_without_knowledge: Plan to go at his usual late-afternoon hour, driving to the cemetery with roses.
